segments.p1-4-g1: This is an onomatopoeic interjection representing laughter; the repeated 'Ха' is simply the laugh broken into beats. The initial dash indicates spoken utterance; there is no grammatical subject or predicate here, only a vocal sound.
segments.p1-4-g2: 'Да' here functions as a colloquial particle or exclamation (hard to render in English) used to express surprise or mild irony; it does not serve as a simple affirmative. 'Вы' is the emphatic second-person pronoun. 'После этого' is a temporal adverbial phrase 'after that' modifying the whole clause. The particle 'и' before the phrase 'в зубной боли' means 'even' (adds emphasis). 'В зубной боли' uses the preposition 'в' + locative/instead here meaning 'in (a) toothache' and 'зубной боли' is a genitive construction 'tooth pain'. 'Отыщете' is the future tense, 2nd person plural of the perfective verb 'отыскать' (a single completed action: 'you will find'), and 'наслаждение' is the direct object 'pleasure'. Word order here (particle + pronoun + adverbial + prepositional phrase + verb + object) is natural Russian for emphasis.
segments.p1-4-g3: 'Вскрикнете' is the future tense, 2nd person plural of the perfective verb 'вскрикнуть' — a single sudden cry: 'you will cry out'. The pronoun 'вы' follows the verb for slight emphasis or rhythm (inversion is stylistic and common in spoken Russian). 'Со' is a variant of the preposition 'с' used before certain consonant clusters, and 'смехом' is the instrumental singular of 'смех' meaning 'with laughter' (instrumental marks accompaniment).
segments.p1-4-g4: The conjunction/contrastive particle 'А' introduces a counterpoint or mild transition (often translated 'and' or 'but' or as a neutral 'well'). 'Что' is the interrogative 'what'. The enclitic particle 'ж' adds emphasis or impatience (roughly 'then' / 'after all'), so together the phrase is a brisk 'And what then?' or 'So what?'.
segments.p1-4-g86: Дескать is a quotative/parenthetical particle used to introduce reported speech or an ironic paraphrase of what others supposedly say; here it frames the following clauses as "so they say" or "as if" and carries an ironic tone.
segments.p1-4-g87: я (I) is the nominative subject; беспокою is 1st person singular present (беспокоить) meaning "I bother/disturb" and takes вас as its direct object (accusative "you"). The clause is a simple present assertion framed as part of reported speech.
segments.p1-4-g88: сердце (heart, here meaning feelings) is the direct object and вам is a dative of the person affected; надрываю is 1st person singular present (надрывать) used metaphorically: literally "I tear/rupture (your) heart," i.e. I cause you great emotional pain.
segments.p1-4-g89: всем в доме is a dative/locative group meaning "to everyone in the house," where всем is the indirect object and в доме specifies location; спать не даю combines the infinitive спать with the negated 1st person singular present даю (here "allow/permit"), literally "I do not allow (them) to sleep," i.e. "I keep everyone in the house awake."
segments.p1-4-g90: Так вот is a discourse marker meaning roughly "so then" or "well then"; не спите is an exhortative negative addressed to the plural/formal you — negative imperative/command "don't sleep" formed with the 2nd person plural form; же adds emphasis or impatience.
segments.p1-4-g91: чувствуйте is the 2nd person plural imperative of чувствовать, an instruction "feel/sense"; же is emphatic; и вы means "and you" or "you too" (и = "also/even"); каждую минуту is an accusative time-expression "every minute" (каждую = "every" modifying минуту = "minute/moment"). The whole clause urges the addressees to be constantly aware of the speaker's pain.
segments.p1-4-g92: что introduces a subordinate clause "that ..."; у меня is the standard Russian possessive/locative construction (literally "at me") used to indicate that the speaker experiences something; зубы is the plural subject "teeth," and болят is the 3rd person plural present of болеть meaning "ache/hurt," so the clause means "that my teeth ache."
segments.p1-4-g93: Word order emphasises contrast: Я (I) + для вас (for you/in your eyes) + уж теперь (already now) and then the predicate не герой (not a hero). There is no copula; Russian omits it in the present. Уж теперь adds a tone of resignation (“by now / already”).
segments.p1-4-g94: Каким is a relative/pronominal form referring back to герой (“what kind of [hero]”), прежде (“formerly/earlier”) modifies хотел (past tense of хотеть, “wanted”), and казаться (infinitive) means “to seem/appear.” Together: “the kind of hero I used to want to seem.”
segments.p1-4-g95: А is contrastive “but”; просто is “simply/merely”; гаденький is a pejorative diminutive adjective “nasty / mean / despicable” (tone of smallness and contempt); человек is the noun “man/person.”
segments.p1-4-g96: Шенапан is a colloquial/archaising label (loaned, used as a noun) meaning a little rogue/rascal or a contemptible fellow; here it stands alone as a dismissive epithet. It is nominative, completing the characterisation.
segments.p1-4-g97: This whole exclamation is colloquial and elliptical. Ну is an interjection/particle meaning “well” that introduces resignation or acceptance. Так is an adverb meaning “then/in that case,” combining with ну to form “well then.” Пусть is the optative/let‑particle used like a 3rd‑person imperative (literally “let it be” or “so be it”) and here is elliptical (the verb that could follow, e.g. быть, is omitted). Же is an emphatic particle attached to the preceding word to strengthen or sharpen the tone; together the phrase expresses reluctant concession or decisive closure.
segments.p1-4-g120: Но is a coordinating conjunction meaning 'but/however' that introduces contrast; ведь is a discourse particle 'after all' that strengthens the explanatory tone; это is a demonstrative pronoun ('this/that/it') functioning as the dummy subject; оттого here is the first part of the causal pairing оттого... что and means 'for that reason / because', so this segment signals 'but/after all, it is for that reason...'.
segments.p1-4-g121: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that/because' introducing the explanatory clause; я is the nominative first-person subject 'I'; сам is an emphatic particle here ('myself / I myself') that stresses the subject; себя is the reflexive accusative 'myself' as the object; не is the negation particle 'not'; уважаю is first-person singular present of уважать (imperfective) meaning 'I respect', so the clause literally reads 'that I myself do not respect myself', i.e. 'that I don't respect myself.'
segments.p1-4-g122: Разве is a particle that opens a rhetorical question (lit. 'really?/is it possible that...'), expressing doubt or incredulity; сознающий is the present active participle/adjectival form of сознавать, describing 'a person who is conscious/aware/admitting' (i.e. 'self-aware'); человек is the noun 'person'; может is the modal verb 'can/is able' in present; сколько-нибудь is the indefinite adverbial 'сколько' + particle 'нибудь' meaning 'to any extent/ever/at all' (softening/limiting, 'even a little'); себя уважать is the reflexive verb phrase 'to respect oneself' with себя as the object and уважать infinitive as the action. The whole asks rhetorically whether a self-aware person can respect himself at all.
segments.p1-4-g98: Subject (Я) + adverb (очень) + short-form adjective (рад) used predicatively: the short form рад expresses the speaker's current emotional state ('I am glad'). The comma prepares for the subordinate clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-4-g99: A subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction что ('that'). Вы is the nominative subject (plural/formal you), меня is the accusative object, and раскусили is the perfective past plural of раскусить meaning 'to see through / figure out' — here a completed action: 'you have seen through me' or 'you figured me out.' Word order is standard S‑O‑V.
segments.p1-4-g100: An impersonal/dative construction: Вам (dative 'to you/for you') indicates the experiencer, скверно is a predicative adverb meaning 'unpleasant' or 'bad,' and the infinitive слушать supplies the activity ('to listen to'). Мои modifies станы (plural), while подленькие is a diminutive/derogatory adjective 'petty/mean' modifying станы ('moans/groans'). The whole is a question addressed to the listeners.
segments.p1-4-g101: Interjection/lead-in Ну так ('Well then') followed by the particle пусть which issues a concessive notion 'let it be' or 'so be it,' and скверно as the predicative adverb 'unpleasant'/'bad.' Together: 'Well then, let it be unpleasant.'
segments.p1-4-g102: Вот is a discourse marker ('now/just/see') introducing the forthcoming action; я (subject) + вам (dative 'for you') + сейчас ('right now') set up timing and beneficiary; ещё + comparative скверней ('even nastier') intensifies the adjective; руладу (accusative) is the object 'roulade' or ornamental phrase; сделаю is the perfective future 'I will make/do' — 'I'll make/give you an even nastier roulade now.' The word order (вот я вам сделаю) places emphasis on the speaker's imminent action.
segments.p1-4-g105: Negative question with the imperfective present понимаете ('you understand' — plural/formal). Не negates the verb; the particle и here functions as an intensifier meaning 'even' ('even now'), and теперь is 'now' — together: 'You don't understand even now.'
segments.p1-4-g106: A vocative/address to the addressees: господа is the plural form used as 'gentlemen' or 'sirs' addressing the audience directly.
segments.p1-4-g19: Это is a demonstrative pronoun introducing the statement; here it functions like English 'this/that (is)'. Хороший пример is a nominative noun phrase (adjective + noun) agreeing in gender/number with пример, so the clause literally reads 'This (is) a good example.'
segments.p1-4-g20: Господа is a form of address (vocative usage in Russian is unstressed and looks like the nominative plural); the comma shows it's direct address: 'gentlemen.'
segments.p1-4-g21: И is the coordinating 'and' linking this clause to the previous. Я is the nominative subject. Его is an accusative object pronoun referring back to 'пример' ('it'); here it means 'it' rather than a possessive. Разовью is first-person singular future (perfective) meaning 'I will develop/expand' — so the verb is future and transitive, taking 'его' as its direct object.
segments.p1-4-g22: В этих стонах is a locative prepositional phrase ('in these moans') with the demonstrative этих agreeing in number/gender with стонах (prep. plural). Выражается is an impersonal/passive-present 3rd-person form meaning 'is expressed' or 'is manifested'; the verb follows the locative phrase for topical emphasis.
segments.p1-4-g23: Во-первых is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'first of all' or 'in the first place' (во here is a variant of в used in the frozen expression).
segments.p1-4-g24: Вся ... бесцельность вашей боли is the noun phrase serving as the semantic content of what's 'expressed': вся (feminine) agrees with бесцельность ('the whole/entire aimlessness'), унизительная is an attributive adjective 'humiliating', для нашего сознания is a prepositional modifier 'for our consciousness/awareness', and вашей боли is a genitive attribute 'of your pain'.
segments.p1-4-g25: Вся законность природы is a second noun phrase coordinated with the previous one ('the whole lawfulness/legality of nature'); законность is used in the abstract sense of 'the law/order (or lawfulness)' of nature, with природы in the genitive.
segments.p1-4-g26: На которую introduces a relative clause modifying законность (feminine), with на + accusative/relative pronoun as the object of the idiomatic predicate наплевать; вам is the dative experiencer 'to you' (literally 'to you it is a matter of spitting on'), so the construction means 'which you don't care about.'
segments.p1-4-g27: Разумеется is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally'; it modifies the following predicate and indicates the speaker's ironic concession.
segments.p1-4-g28: Наплевать is an impersonal/coarse verb meaning 'to not care' (literally 'to spit on'); in usage it takes a dative experiencer (вам) and a prepositional object (на + accusative), here expressing 'you couldn't care less.'
segments.p1-4-g29: Но is the adversative 'but'. От которой begins a relative clause with от ('from/because of') + relative pronoun referring back to природа/законность, so 'from which.' Вы is the subject 'you'; все-таки is a fixed adverb meaning 'nevertheless/still' (here split into two words in the text); страдаете is 2nd-person plural present meaning 'you suffer' — with от it means 'suffer from' (i.e., you are harmed by it even though you don't care).
segments.p1-4-g30: А is a contrastive 'but/while.' Она-то uses the stressed particle -то on the pronoun она to emphasize the contrast ('but it, on the other hand, ...'). Нет is a short, colloquial negation meaning 'does not (do so)' in this elliptical clause: 'but it does not (suffer).'
segments.p1-4-g31: Выражается is the present passive/reflexive form of выражаться (3rd singular) and here functions impersonally: “is expressed” or “is shown.” Сознание is nominative and the subject of that impersonal construction (literally, “an awareness is expressed”).
segments.p1-4-g32: Что introduces a subordinate clause meaning “that.” Врага у вас не находится is an existential construction: находится (3rd sg) “is found/located,” negated by не; у вас marks the person (literally “at you” → “you don’t/you have not”), and врага (animate singular form) denotes the absent object “an enemy.” Word order is inverted for emphasis.
segments.p1-4-g33: А is contrastive “but/and,” and что again introduces the clause. Боль есть uses the present copula есть to assert existence: “there is pain” (more emphatic than a bare noun).
segments.p1-4-g34: Сознание repeats the noun as a resumed subject for the following series of clauses (an appositive repetition): “(there is) an awareness” — the sentence lists several things this awareness contains.
segments.p1-4-g35: Что introduces the next clause (“that”), and вы is the nominative subject “you” (plural or formal singular) of that clause.
segments.p1-4-g36: Со + instrumental plural marks accompaniment: со всевозможными Вагенгеймами = “with all sorts of Wagenheims.” Со is the variant of с used before certain consonant clusters; всевозможными is instrumental plural (”all possible/kinds of”), and Вагенгеймами is the proper name in instrumental plural.
segments.p1-4-g37: Вполне (adverb) modifies the phrase в рабстве (“in slavery/servitude”); у ваших зубов uses у + genitive to indicate being 'at/by' or 'under the power of' someone/something: “in slavery to your teeth.” The genitive ваших зубов shows whose power.
segments.p1-4-g38: Что again introduces a content clause. Захочет is the perfective future (3rd sg) of захотеть (“will want/will decide”); кто-то is the indefinite pronoun “someone” (кто + particle то). The clause sets up a conditional sense: “if someone wants/decides.”
segments.p1-4-g39: И connects clauses; перестанут (future, 3rd pl) + infinitive болеть = “will stop aching.” Ваши зубы is the nominative plural subject (“your teeth”).
segments.p1-4-g40: А introduces the counterfactual/contrast (“but/and if not”) and не negates the verb: не захочет = “if he does not want/will not will (it).” The verb remains future 3rd singular.
segments.p1-4-g41: Так и gives the sense “and so / in that case” with emphasis on continuation; ещё три месяца is the duration “another three months” (ещё = still/another), and проболят is the perfective future (3rd pl) of проболеть meaning “to be ill/ache for (a period)” — “they will continue to ache for three more months.”
segments.p1-4-g42: И что again links to the next item in the list (“and that”). It simply introduces another clause continuing the enumeration of what is felt/realized.
segments.p1-4-g43: Наконец is an adverb meaning “finally / at last,” preparing the concluding point of the enumeration.
segments.p1-4-g44: Если begins a conditional “if.” Вы is subject; все ещё (все + ещё) here means “still” (все often reinforces extent), несогласны is a predicate adjective in plural (“are not in agreement/dissenting”), и все-таки uses the particle таки to mean “nevertheless/after all,” and протестуете is 2nd pl (or formal 2nd sg) future/present of протестовать “to protest.” Note two different 'все' tokens: the first (все ещё) acts adverbially “still,” the second (все-таки) is part of the particle meaning “nevertheless.”
segments.p1-4-g45: То picks up the conditional (“then”); вам остаётся is an impersonal/dative construction: “to you remains / is left”; для собственного утешения = “for your own consolation” (для + genitive), только самого себя высечь = “only to flog/whip oneself” (самого себя emphasises oneself in the accusative), или прибить побольнее кулаком вашу стену = “or to strike/nail/beat your wall with the fist rather more painfully” (кулаком = instrumental). The infinitives высечь / прибить express what action remains available.
segments.p1-4-g46: А contrasts and summarizes: более решительно is a comparative adverbial phrase “more decisively,” and ничего is the indefinite pronoun “nothing” (here meaning ‘nothing else’); together they mean “and nothing more decisive” — i.e., nothing further can be done.
segments.p1-4-g47: Ну-с is an interjection/colloquial tag meaning “well then” or “well now.” Ну is the interjection; the appended -с (a variant of с) gives the colloquial force “well, then.”
segments.p1-4-g48: Вот от этих-то кровавых обид: вот is a deictic/emphatic particle (“now/just/from those”); от + genitive (этих) = “from these,” and the enclitic -то on этих (этих-то) adds emphasis/contrast; кровавых is genitive plural adjective “bloody,” обид is the noun in genitive plural (“offences/grievances”).
segments.p1-4-g49: Вот от этих-то насмешек repeats the pattern: “from those very mockeries.” Насмешек is genitive plural of насмешка (“mockery”), again emphasized by этих-то.
segments.p1-4-g50: Неизвестно чьих is an attributive phrase: неизвестно functions as a predicative/adverbial “unknown” and чьих is the genitive plural of чей “whose,” so together “of unknown whose” → “whose (origin) is unknown.”
segments.p1-4-g51: И начинается наконец наслаждение: и links, начинается is present (3rd sg) “begins/starts,” наконец “at last/finally,” and наслаждение is the nominative noun “pleasure” which is the subject that begins.
segments.p1-4-g52: Доходящее is a present participle (agreeing with наслаждение) meaning “reaching” or “that reaches” and specifies the extent of the pleasure; иногда is “sometimes,” до + genitive (высшего сладострастия) indicates extent/limit (“up to / as far as the highest sensuality”), with высшего qualifying сладострастия (“the highest sensuality/voluptuousness”).
segments.p1-4-g5: и functions here as an emphatic 'even' (not just a plain 'and'); в + noun in the phrase в зубной боли uses the prepositional case (зубной — feminine prepositional/ genitive form of the adjective) to mean 'in tooth pain' or 'in a toothache'; есть is the present form of быть used existentially ('there is'); наслаждение is the noun 'pleasure' and is the thing whose existence is asserted.
segments.p1-4-g6: отвечу is the 1st person singular, perfective future form of ответить ('I will answer'), here used parenthetically as the speaker's retort; я is the subject pronoun 'I' and simply identifies the speaker.
segments.p1-4-g7: У меня is the dative/possessive construction meaning 'I have' or 'at me' (here 'I had / to me there was'); целый месяц gives duration 'a whole month' (целый emphasises the entirety); болели is the past plural of болеть because the grammatical subject зубы (teeth) is plural — meaning 'ached'; зубы is the plural subject 'teeth'.
segments.p1-4-g8: я знаю is a simple present statement 'I know' used to assert the speaker's awareness; the comma sets up the following subordinate clause.
segments.p1-4-g9: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that'; есть again is existential 'there is' and here is elliptical — the object (pleasure) is understood from the previous sentence ('I know that there is').
segments.p1-4-g10: Тут is a colloquial adverb meaning 'here' or 'at this point' — it sets the scene or location of the action.
segments.p1-4-g11: конечно is the adverb 'of course' (a comment/admission that what follows is to be expected).
segments.p1-4-g12: не is the negation 'not'; молча is the adverb 'silently'; злятся is the 3rd person plural present reflexive form of злиться — 'they get/become angry' (here negated: 'they do not get angry silently').
segments.p1-4-g13: а is a contrasting conjunction 'but/while'; стонут is 3rd person plural present of стонать 'they moan' — the contrast is between quiet anger and audible moaning.
segments.p1-4-g14: но is another adversative 'but/however'; это here functions as a demonstrative 'these/this'; стоны is the plural noun 'moans'; не negates откровенные, and откровенные is the plural adjective 'candid' or 'frank' — 'these moans are not candid.'
segments.p1-4-g15: это повторяет 'these' (this very thing); стоны 'moans' again; с is the preposition 'with'; ехидством is the noun in the instrumental case meaning 'with malice' or 'with malicious mockery' — instrumental here expresses the accompanying quality of the moans.
segments.p1-4-g16: а is 'and/but' introducing the concluding point; в ехидстве is the prepositional form 'in the malice/mockery'; то is the emphatic particle added to ехидстве (этих-то, ехидстве-то) to mean 'precisely/just in'; и here is an intensifier ('indeed' / 'that is'); вся штука literally 'the whole thing' — idiomatically 'that's the whole point.'
segments.p1-4-g17: В is the preposition 'in'; этих-то is the demonstrative these in the prepositional plural with the emphatic particle -то ('these very'); стонах is prepositional plural of стон 'moans'; и here adds emphasis ('indeed/even'); выражается is the reflexive/passive form 'is expressed' (the pleasure is expressed); наслаждение is 'pleasure'; страдающего is the present-participle/ adjectival form of страдать in the genitive singular, functioning as 'of the sufferer' ('the sufferer's pleasure').
segments.p1-4-g18: не is negation; ощущал is past tense 'felt/perceived' used here with the conditional particle бы to form 'would feel'; бы is the conditional particle; он is 'he'; в них means 'in them' (in those moans); наслаждения is the partitive/genitive 'pleasure' (object of ощущал); the dash separates the conditional result: он бы и стонать не стал — он бы (conditional) plus стонать (infinitive) plus не стал (past of стать with бы forming conditional) yields 'he would not even begin to moan' (и functions as 'even' here).
segments.p1-4-g107: «Нет,» is an interjection here meaning 'no' or 'well, no' that rejects or corrects the preceding thought; it stands alone as a parenthetical response and is set off by a comma.
segments.p1-4-g108: «видно,» is an impersonal adverb meaning 'apparently' or 'it seems' and functions as a parenthetical comment that qualifies the speaker's claim; it is commonly followed by a comma.
segments.p1-4-g109: «надо» is an impersonal modal word ('one must/it is necessary') that governs the following infinitives; «глубоко» is an adverb modifying both coordinated perfective reflexive infinitives «доразвиться» and «досознаться» (prefix до- here marks completing/developing to a certain point, and the -ся makes them reflexive/passive in sense 'develop oneself / become aware').
segments.p1-4-g110: «чтоб» (colloquial contraction of чтобы) introduces purpose ('in order that'); it is followed by the infinitive «понять» ('to understand') and its direct object «все изгибы» (accusative plural). «этого сладострастия» is a genitive attribute ('of this sensuality'), so the whole phrase means 'in order to understand all the bends/twists of this sensuality.'
segments.p1-4-g111: «Вы» is the 2nd-person plural or polite singular pronoun (subject). «смеетесь» is the 2nd-person plural present indicative of the reflexive verb «смеяться» ('to laugh'); together they form a direct interrogative 'Are you laughing?' which can be rhetorical.
segments.p1-4-g112: «Очень» is an adverb meaning 'very'. «рад» is the short-form predicate adjective meaning 'glad' (masculine); the clipped form «рад-с» is a colloquial/archaic stylistic ending attached here (Dostoevsky's usage) and does not change the basic predicative meaning 'I am glad.'
segments.p1-4-g113: «Мои» is the possessive pronoun 'my' in plural nominative modifying «шутки» (nominative plural), which is the subject of the sentence: 'my jokes.'
segments.p1-4-g114: «господа» is the vocative or direct address 'gentlemen' (plural); here it addresses the listeners and is set off by commas.
segments.p1-4-g115: «конечно,» is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'certainly', commenting on the following characterization.
segments.p1-4-g116: «дурного тона» is a noun phrase meaning 'of bad tone/manner' (literally 'of bad tone'); «дурного» is an adjective in the genitive singular agreeing with «тона» (genitive of тон), and the whole phrase functions as a qualitative attribute—i.e. 'in bad taste' or 'of bad tone.'
segments.p1-4-g117: «неровны,» is the short-form plural adjective «неровный» used predicatively and agreeing with the plural subject «шутки»; it means 'uneven' or 'inconsistent' (here describing the jokes' quality/structure).
segments.p1-4-g118: «сбивчивы,» is another short-form plural adjective (from «сбивчивый») used predicatively and agreeing with «шутки»; it means 'disjointed' or 'confused'—describing how the jokes come out.
segments.p1-4-g119: «с» is the preposition 'with' and governs the instrumental; «самонедоверчивостью» is the noun 'self-mistrust' in the instrumental singular, so the phrase means 'with self-mistrust'—i.e. the jokes are accompanied by or characterized by self-distrust.
segments.p1-4-g53: 'Я вас прошу' is first-person singular present indicative of просить with 'вас' in the accusative: literally 'I ask you'. Here it functions idiomatically as a polite/earnest entreaty ('I beg you').
segments.p1-4-g54: The single vocative address 'господа' addresses the audience ('gentlemen'). It's a direct form of address set off by the preceding comma.
segments.p1-4-g55: 'Прислушайтесь' is the perfective imperative plural (or polite plural) of прислушаться: 'listen closely (to)'. 'Когда-нибудь' (split here into 'когда' + 'нибудь') is an indefinite temporal adverb meaning 'sometime/ever'. 'К стонам' is dative plural governed by прислушайтесь ('to the moans'). The phrase 'образованного человека девятнадцатого столетия' is a genitive phrase modifying 'стонам' ('the moans of an educated man of the nineteenth century').
segments.p1-4-g56: 'Страдающего' is a present participle in the genitive singular agreeing with 'человека' (it further describes the man). With the verb 'страдать' here the following instrument 'зубами' is used in the same idiom as English 'suffering from the teeth' (i.e., having toothache); 'зубами' is instrumental.
segments.p1-4-g57: 'Этак' is colloquial and here means roughly 'say' or 'about' (giving an approximate timing). 'На второй или на третий день болезни' is a temporal expression: 'на' + accusative 'день' indicates the point in time ('on the second or third day of the illness'); 'болезни' is genitive.
segments.p1-4-g58: Subordinate temporal clause introduced by 'когда' ('when'). 'Он начинает' is present-tense 'he begins', followed by the infinitive 'стонать' ('to moan'); 'уже' = 'already'. The negation 'не так' sets up the contrast that follows: 'no longer/ not in the same way.'
segments.p1-4-g59: 'Как в первый день стонал' is a comparative clause ('as he moaned on the first day') with 'стонал' in the masculine past tense describing the earlier manner of moaning.
segments.p1-4-g60: 'То есть' is a fixed phrase meaning 'that is' or 'i.e.' and introduces clarification. 'Не просто оттого' negates a simple causal explanation: 'not simply because/for the reason that...'.
segments.p1-4-g61: The subordinate clause 'что зубы болят' gives the reason that is being rejected: 'that the teeth hurt.' 'Зубы' is the subject (nominative plural) and 'болят' is the present-tense plural of 'болеть' meaning 'ache'.
segments.p1-4-g62: Short elliptical phrase 'не так' (= 'not in that way'), preparing a contrast with what follows.
segments.p1-4-g63: Comparative 'как какой-нибудь грубый мужик' = 'not like some coarse peasant/man.' 'Как' introduces the comparison; 'какой-нибудь' is an indefinite (dismissive) modifier 'some/any'; 'мужик' = 'peasant' or coarse fellow.
segments.p1-4-g64: 'А так' is a contrastive particle pair ('but rather so/like this') linking to the forthcoming alternative description.
segments.p1-4-g65: Comparative clause 'как человек тронутый развитием и европейской цивилизацией стонет' literally 'as a man touched by development and European civilization moans.' 'Тронутый' is a past participle postpositive to 'человек'; 'развитием' and 'европейской цивилизацией' are instrumental, indicating what has 'moved' or affected him; 'стонет' is present tense 'moans.'
segments.p1-4-g66: Reiterative 'как человек' ('as a man') introduces the following quoted characterization; here it functions as a resumptive simile.
segments.p1-4-g67: Quoted participial phrase 'отрешившийся от почвы и народных начал' is a past active participle meaning 'having detached himself/withdrawn from the soil and the people's beginnings' — a critical formula. 'От' governs the genitive 'почвы'; 'народных начал' is genitive plural meaning 'popular/basal beginnings.'
segments.p1-4-g68: 'Как теперь выражаются' is a parenthetical comment: 'as they express it now' or 'as people nowadays say.' 'Выражаются' is the reflexive/passive plural present of выражать — 'to be expressed / to put it.'
segments.p1-4-g69: 'Стоны его' = 'his moans' (possessive 'его' modifying 'стоны'). 'Становятся' is the present-tense plural of становиться — 'become.' 'Какие-то' is an indefinite, colloquial qualifier ('some kind of' / 'somehow') and 'скверные' is the adjective 'nasty/bad.' Together they convey 'take on a kind of nasty quality.'
segments.p1-4-g70: 'Пакостно-злые' is a compound adjective (adverb + adjective) meaning 'nasty-spiteful' or 'mischievously angry.' 'И продолжаются' links with the preceding predicate: 'and continue.' 'По целым дням и ночам' uses 'по' + dative to indicate duration/frequency: 'for whole days and nights' (i.e., for days and nights on end).
segments.p1-4-g71: The particles 'И ведь' add emphasis/contrast ('and yet/after all'). 'Знает' is present tense 'he knows'; 'сам' is an emphatic pronoun here meaning 'he himself' (emphasis on the subject).
segments.p1-4-g72: Subordinate clause introduced by 'что' ('that') stating the content of his knowledge. 'Никакой себе пользы не принесет стонами' — 'will bring him no benefit by moaning.' 'Никакой' negates 'пользы' and 'стонами' (instrumental) indicates the means/way.
segments.p1-4-g73: 'Лучше всех знает' is an idiomatic comparative: 'knows better than anyone.' 'Лучше' is the comparative 'better' and 'всех' is the genitive plural 'of all', making the comparative construction.
segments.p1-4-g74: Another 'that'-clause: 'что он только напрасно себя и других надрывает и раздражает' — 'that he only in vain overstrains/exhausts himself and others and annoys them.' 'Надрывает' here means 'wears out/exhausts' (figurative) and the verbs are present-tense, habitual or general.
segments.p1-4-g75: A brief resumptive 'знает' (he knows), repeating the verb to introduce the next series of facts he is aware of.
segments.p1-4-g76: 'Что даже и публика' begins another content clause: 'that even the public...' The particles 'даже и' add emphasis: 'even.'
segments.p1-4-g77: 'Перед которой он старается' is a relative clause modifying 'публика' ('the public before whom he strives/attempts'); 'перед' + relative 'которой' marks the audience in front of whom he is performing or trying to please, and 'старается' is a reflexive present meaning 'tries' or 'makes an effort.'
segments.p1-4-g78: 'И все семейство его уже прислушались к нему с омерзением' — 'and his entire household have already listened to him with disgust.' Grammatically, 'прислушались' is perfective past plural 'listened/attended (with dislike)', 'к нему' indicates 'to him', and 'с омерзением' is an instrumental phrase 'with disgust.'
segments.p1-4-g79: 'Не верят ему ни на грош' is an idiom meaning 'do not believe him even a penny' (i.e., don't believe him at all). 'Не верят' is present plural, 'ему' dative. 'Понимают про себя' means 'they understand to themselves' — 'they privately realize.'
segments.p1-4-g80: 'Что он мог бы иначе' introduces a modal possibility: 'that he could otherwise' — 'мог' is past-tense stem used with 'бы' to form the conditional 'could.' 'Иначе' = 'differently/otherwise.'
segments.p1-4-g81: 'Проще стонать' is an infinitival complement meaning 'to moan more simply' or 'moan in a simpler way.' It completes the conditional 'мог бы' ('could').
segments.p1-4-g82: 'Без рулад и без вывертов' is a paired prepositional phrase 'without roulades and without tricks'; 'без' governs the genitive and enumerates the ostentatious flourishes he could do without.
segments.p1-4-g83: 'А что он только так со злости' introduces the explanation: 'but that he only does it like that out of anger.' 'Со злости' is an idiomatic instrumental phrase meaning 'from anger' or 'out of spite.'
segments.p1-4-g84: 'С ехидства балуется' finishes the clause: 'he is only amusing himself with it out of malice/mischief.' 'Балуется' is reflexive and here means 'indulges/plays at' (treats it as a pastime or trifle), and 'с ехидства' ('from malice') gives the motive.
segments.p1-4-g85: Discourse marker 'Ну так вот' combines particles to draw a conclusion ('well then' / 'so'). 'В этих-то всех' places emphasis on 'these very' (the particle '-то' adds insistence) and 'сознаниях и позорах' are in the prepositional plural ('in these consciences and shames'). 'И заключается сладострастие' uses заключаться (present passive/reflexive) to mean 'consists in / is contained in' — 'the sensual pleasure consists in them.'

sentences.p1-4-s2: Why, after that you'll even find pleasure in a toothache!
sentences.p1-4-s3: You will cry out with laughter.
sentences.p1-4-s17: "They say I'm troubling you, tearing your heart, and not letting anyone in the house sleep."
sentences.p1-4-s18: "So then, don't sleep; do feel, you too, every minute that my teeth are aching."
sentences.p1-4-s19: I am no longer, for you, the hero I formerly wanted to seem, but simply a nasty little man — a shenapan.
sentences.p1-4-s31: But after all, that's because I don't respect myself.
sentences.p1-4-s32: Can a self-aware person respect himself even a little?
sentences.p1-4-s21: I'm very glad that you've seen through me.
sentences.p1-4-s22: Is it unpleasant for you to listen to my petty little moans?
sentences.p1-4-s23: All right — let it be unpleasant; I'll give you an even nastier roulade right now.
sentences.p1-4-s26: Don't you understand even now, gentlemen?
sentences.p1-4-s9: That's a good example, gentlemen, and I'll develop it.
sentences.p1-4-s10: In these moans is expressed, first of all, the whole humiliating aimlessness of your pain; the entire lawfulness of nature, which, of course, you couldn't care less about, but from which you nevertheless suffer — while it does not.
sentences.p1-4-s11: There is an awareness expressed that you have no enemy, but that there is pain; an awareness that you, together with all sorts of Wagenheims, are entirely in slavery to your teeth; that if someone wills it your teeth will stop aching, and if he does not, they will continue to ache for another three months; and that, finally, if you still disagree and nevertheless protest, all that remains for your own consolation is to flog yourself or to strike your wall harder with your fist — nothing more decisive.
sentences.p1-4-s12: Well then, from those very bloody insults, from those very mockeries of unknown origin, there at last begins a pleasure that sometimes reaches the highest voluptuousness.
sentences.p1-4-s5: And even in a toothache there is pleasure, I will answer.
sentences.p1-4-s6: My teeth ached for a whole month; I know that there is.
sentences.p1-4-s7: Here, of course, they do not grow angry silently, but moan; however these moans are not candid, these are moans with malice, and it is in that malice that the whole point lies.
sentences.p1-4-s8: It is in these very moans that the sufferer's pleasure is expressed; if he did not feel pleasure in them, he would not even begin to moan.
sentences.p1-4-s27: No, apparently one must develop oneself deeply and come to full awareness in order to understand all the twists of this sensuality!
sentences.p1-4-s30: My jokes, gentlemen, are of course in bad taste, uneven, confused, and full of self-mistrust.
sentences.p1-4-s13: I beg you, gentlemen, listen sometime to the moans of an educated man of the nineteenth century, suffering from his teeth, say on the second or third day of illness, when he already begins to groan not in the way he groaned on the first day—that is, not simply because his teeth hurt; not like some coarse peasant, but as a man touched by development and European civilization moans, as a man "detached from the soil and popular beginnings," as they put it nowadays.
sentences.p1-4-s14: His moans become somehow nasty, wickedly spiteful, and go on for whole days and nights.
sentences.p1-4-s15: And yet he himself knows that groaning will bring him no benefit; he knows better than anyone that he only exhausts and irritates himself and others in vain; he knows that even the public he is trying to please, and the whole household, have already listened to him with disgust, do not believe him a penny, and privately understand that he could groan otherwise, more simply, without roulades and without tricks, but that he is only playing at it out of anger and malice.
sentences.p1-4-s16: Well then, it is precisely in all these consciences and disgraces that the sensual pleasure consists.
